t_ch: …很简单，这颗星核与我们无关，但仙舟已经把罪责安在了星核猎手头上；
t_en: ...It's simple. That Stellaron has nothing to do with us, but the Xianzhou is convinced that we're responsible.

t_ch: 我的同伴刃被云骑军带走了，我要把他带回来，解除这次的星核危机，洗脱我们的嫌疑。
t_en: My companion Blade has been taken away by the Cloud Knights. I want to bring him back, resolve this Stellaron crisis, and clear our names.

t_ch: 与你们无关？谁信啊，哪有这么巧的事情，星核刚爆发你就出现了……
t_en: Nothing to do with you? Yeah right — sounds like a weird coincidence to me. You just happened to show up after the Stellaron burst...?

t_ch: 再说，你和我们又没什么交情，星核猎手清不清白和咱们有什么关系~
t_en: Also, we're not your friends. Why should we care if a Stellaron Hunter is innocent, hmm~?

t_ch: 我们应该封印星核，帮助仙舟。
t_en: We should seal the Stellaron and help the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 我们可以卖星核猎手一个人情。
t_en: Maybe we can sell our friendship to the Stellaron Hunters.

t_ch: 这么做对我们有什么好处？
t_en: Is there anything in this for us?

t_ch: 不要！我才不想听她的——联盟那么厉害，难道还处理不了一颗星核嘛！我们是星穹列车组，又不是星核封印专业户！
t_en: No! Why are we listening to her? The Alliance is strong — is a single Stellaron burst really too much for them to handle? We're the crew of the Astral Express, not some squad of Stellaron-sealing super specialists!

t_ch: 你们当然可以置身事外。趁现在星核还没污染这片空域，启动跃迁，你们就可以去下一个世界。但假以时日，这段星轨将再度被阻断。
t_en: You could, of course, not get involved. Knowing that the Stellaron hasn't yet affected this region of space, you could make the jump and arrive at the next world. But sooner or later, the star rail here will be more blocked off than it was before.

t_ch: 我可以告诉你们未来会如何：如果你们没有前往「罗浮」，星核最终将污染整艘仙舟，飞船上大约一半的住民将会丧生……
t_en: I can tell you what the future holds: If you don't go to the Luofu, the Stellaron will eventually contaminate the entire ship, and over half of the inhabitants will perish.

t_ch: 勇敢无畏的开拓者，天行为善的无名客，想来不会坐视不理。
t_en: Oh you brave and fearless Trailblazers, you benevolent Nameless, can you really remain indifferent to that?

t_ch: …你的那位同伴，叫刃的，也无法幸免，对吗？
t_en: ...This companion of yours, Blade, he'd perish too, right?

t_ch: 这一点嘛，无可奉告。
t_en: No comment.

t_ch: 坐标在这里，一切交由你们自己决定。
t_en: Here are the coordinates. It's up to you how to proceed.

t_ch: 虽然追求的目标截然不同，但群星的轨道终将彼此交汇。再见。
t_en: Our destinations may differ, but the orbits of the stars will eventually converge. See you later.

t_ch: 三月，把丹恒找来。
t_en: ...March, get Dan Heng.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你有什么想法吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what do you think about all this?

t_ch: 应该去仙舟。
t_en: We should go to the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 是吗？你的理由是？
t_en: Oh? And why is that?

t_ch: 因为我们是无名客。
t_en: Because we're the Nameless.

t_ch: 哈哈，你已经很有开拓者的样子了。没错，总不能对身处危机的人置之不理吧。
t_en: Haha, I see you're already a full-fledged Trailblazer. You're right, we can't remain indifferent to those in crisis.

t_ch: 因为仙舟需要帮助。
t_en: Because the Xianzhou needs us.

t_ch: 哈哈，你已经很有开拓者的样子了。没错，总不能对身处危机的人置之不理吧。
t_en: Haha, I see you're already a full-fledged Trailblazer. You're right, we can't remain indifferent to those in crisis.

t_ch: 因为我们不去不行。
t_en: Because we can't not go.

t_ch: 卡芙卡的话也不能尽信。虽然「星核猎手老大能预知未来」的可信度很高，但卡芙卡未必说了实话。
t_en: We can't trust everything Kafka says. It's credible that the leader of the Stellaron Hunters can predict the future, but Kafka might not be telling the truth.

t_ch: …这也是件麻烦的事。如果她所说属实，而我们又置若罔闻，「罗浮」上的人就会遭遇危险。
t_en: ...This is a difficult matter. If she is telling the truth and we turn a deaf ear, then those on the Luofu will be in great danger.

t_ch: 不能去仙舟。
t_en: We can't go to the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 哦？为什么？
t_en: Oh? How come?

t_ch: 卡芙卡的话不能信。
t_en: We can't trust what Kafka says.

t_ch: 是的，虽然「星核猎手老大能预知未来」的可信度很高，但卡芙卡未必说了实话。
t_en: You're right. It's credible that the leader of the Stellaron Hunters can predict the future, but Kafka might not be telling the truth.

t_ch: …这也是件麻烦的事。如果她所说属实，而我们又置若罔闻，「罗浮」上的人就会遭遇危险。
t_en: ...This is a difficult matter. If she is telling the truth and we turn a deaf ear, then those on the Luofu will be in great danger.

t_ch: 星核猎手别有所图。
t_en: The Stellaron Hunters are up to something.

t_ch: 想来如此。我有见过公司关于「刃」的情报，如果内容属实，他极度危险。
t_en: It wouldn't be surprising. I've seen the IPC's file on Blade. If the details are accurate, he's incredibly dangerous.

t_ch: 很难相信这样的人被云骑军抓住，星核猎手却要来找我们出手相助。
t_en: It's hard to believe that someone like him could be captured by the Cloud Knights and that the Stellaron Hunters would look to us for help.

t_ch: 这事和我们没关系。
t_en: This has nothing to do with us.

t_ch: 是啊，但…如果她所说属实，而我们又置若罔闻，「罗浮」上的人就会遭遇危险。
t_en: Yes, however... if she is telling the truth and we turn a deaf ear, then those on the Luofu will be in great danger.

t_ch: 我都行啦。
t_en: I'm okay with whatever we do.

t_ch: 你的心态可真好呀。
t_en: I like the way you think.

t_ch: 对了{NICKNAME}，卡芙卡这次现身，完全没提和你有关的事呢……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, did you notice that Kafka didn't mention anything to do with you...?

t_ch: 也许「刃」更重要吧。
t_en: Maybe Blade's more important.

t_ch: 我和她已经没关系了。
t_en: I have nothing to do with her anymore.

t_ch: 总觉得她是故意的。
t_en: It seemed like she was avoiding it.
Hmm...

t_ch: 星核猎手往你身体里塞了颗星核，这一举动必然有长远的用意。人人都说艾利欧能够预见未来，他的每步谋划都是伏笔。
t_en: A Stellaron Hunter implanted you with a Stellaron — a move like that hints at a far-off purpose. Everyone says that Elio can foresee the future, and that every step of his schemes is a foreshadowing.

t_ch: 你要多加小心，{NICKNAME}。卡芙卡不会永远对你视而不见。
t_en: You must be extra vigilant, {NICKNAME}. Kafka won't always turn a blind eye.

t_ch: 仙舟联盟…我只听说过，还没打过交道呢。
t_en: The Xianzhou Alliance... I've heard of them, but never had any contact with them.

t_ch: 强大的仙舟联盟，怎么会被一颗星核攻破呢……
t_en: How could the mighty Xianzhou Alliance be breached by a Stellaron...

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生怎么看？
t_en: What do you make of it, Mr. Yang?

t_ch: 事有蹊跷。仙舟联盟是为数不多直接从星神那里汲取力量的派系，他们的实力非常强大，远远超过已逝的阿基维利庇佑下的列车。
t_en: Something's not right. The Xianzhou Alliance is a small faction, but one that derives its power directly from an Aeon. They have immense strength — much more than a train under the protection of the fallen Akivili.

t_ch: 散落在银河里的星星会被「星核」的危机击垮，但仙舟拥有「巡猎」的岚啊，我不觉得星核在星神眼中会是什么麻烦……
t_en: Stellarons can lead to the collapse of stars scattered throughout the universe, but the Xianzhou has Lan, The Hunt on their side. I struggle to believe that Stellarons pose a threat in the eyes of the Aeons.

t_ch: 星核猎手说谎，或者…仙舟「罗浮」上隐藏着更大的威胁……
t_en: Either the Stellaron Hunters are lying, or... there's an even bigger threat lurking on the Xianzhou Luofu...

t_ch: 仙舟联盟很厉害吗？
t_en: Is the Xianzhou Alliance really that powerful?

t_ch: 可以说是宇宙中已知势力里排得上号的存在了。
t_en: It's fair to say that it ranks highly among the known forces in the universe.

t_ch: 「巡猎」的命途相对狭隘，在星神中并不算突出，但联盟毕竟拥有祂的直接赐福。六艘仙舟互为倚仗，同气连枝。
t_en: The Path of The Hunt is narrow and doesn't stand out among the other Paths, but the Alliance enjoys the direct blessing of the Aeon. The six great ships of the Xianzhou are like brothers in arms.

t_ch: 那些仅仅从命途中汲取力量的派系，没法与之相比。
t_en: Its power is much greater than those factions that simply draw their strength from the Paths themselves.

t_ch: 事关重大，这次我也要参与行动。
t_en: This is big. I'm coming with you this time.

t_ch: 事关重大，这次我也要参与行动。
t_en: This is big. I'm coming with you this time.

t_ch: 还想听听您的看法。
t_en: I want to hear your thoughts.

t_ch: 事有蹊跷，仙舟受到「巡猎」的庇佑，我不觉得星核在星神眼中会是什么麻烦……
t_en: Something's not right — the Xianzhou has Lan The Hunt's blessing. I struggle to believe that Stellarons pose a threat in the eyes of the Aeons...

t_ch: 所以，要么星核猎手说谎，要么仙舟「罗浮」上隐藏着更大的威胁。
t_en: ...Which means either the Stellaron Hunters are lying, or... there's an even bigger threat lurking on the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 想了解下仙舟联盟。
t_en: I want to know more about the Xianzhou Alliance.

t_ch: 联盟是由六艘漂航巨舰「仙舟」组成的同盟，他们追随「巡猎」的岚，以消灭星神「丰饶」为使命。
t_en: The Alliance is made up of six great ships, all following Lan, The Hunt. Its mission is to eliminate the Aeon of Abundance.

t_ch: 在星神之中，岚并不强大，祂的命途概念「巡猎」比较狭隘…但是，有别于大多数星神，岚非常关注凡人，祂几乎毫无保留地将力量交给仙舟联盟。
t_en: Lan doesn't rank among the more powerful Aeons — the concept of the Path of The Hunt is narrow... but in contrast with the other Aeons, THEY pay close attention to mortals. Lan grants strength to the Xianzhou Alliance without reservation.

t_ch: 因此，联盟的「元帅」和六「将军」并不比军团的「绝灭大君」逊色。
t_en: It follows that the Alliance's Marshal and six Generals are in no way inferior to Lord Ravagers.

t_ch: 不打扰您了。
t_en: I won't disturb you.

t_ch: 原本的目的地能透露吗？
t_en: Are you allowed to divulge our original destination...?

t_ch: 现在告诉你应该没关系了…原本要去的地方叫匹诺康尼，在列车的记载里，它是公司用来流放罪犯的监狱星——至少阿基维利时代是这样的。
t_en: I guess it doesn't matter if I tell you now... Our original destination was a place called Penacony. The records on the Express indicate that it's a prison planet used by the IPC to exile criminals — at least that's how it was in the days of Akivili.

t_ch: 万界之癌爆发的时候，公司失去了对它的控制，于是匹诺康尼投入了「同谐」希佩的怀抱，现在它是「家族」的一员了。
t_en: When the Cancer of All Worlds broke out, the IPC lost control of Penacony, and the planet sought refuge under Xipe the Harmony. Now it's a member of "The Family."

t_ch: 匹诺康尼正在举办一场盛会，因此「家族」给列车发来了邀请函。姬子打算接受邀请，所以这次，我们不是作为开拓者，而是以客人的身份前去赴宴。
t_en: Penacony is holding an event, and The Family sent an invitation to the Express. Himeko intends to accept the invitation, so we were planning to travel there as guests, not Trailblazers.

t_ch: …结果，唉，得调头先去仙舟了。
t_en: ...But alas, for now we're changing our bearings and heading for the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 卡芙卡…唔，帕姆不喜欢她，有一股危险的味道。
t_en: Kafka... *sigh* Pom-Pom doesn't like her. She smells of danger.

t_ch: 不过，开拓者的道路和目标，都要由开拓者自己选择。这是阿基维利大人的信条。
t_en: Still, a Trailblazer should choose their goal and the road they take to get there themselves. That's the creed of the great Akivili.

t_ch: 哇，你怎么也来了。
t_en: Whoa, what are you doing here?

t_ch: 人咱已经叫出来啦，发生的事也跟他讲了一遍。喂，说好了啊，待会儿咱们统一投反对票！我才不想听那个女人的指示哩。
t_en: I just gave him the low-down. Hey, remember what I said, okay? We're gonna vote against it! I'm not gonna take orders from that woman.

t_ch: 我被禁止踏入罗浮。「凡所治处，不得履踏」…回主车厢去吧。
t_en: I'm forbidden from setting foot on the Luofu. "Total interception of any attempt to enter the dominion." *sigh* Let's head back to the main carriage.

t_ch: 哪有那么巧的事，星核爆发的地方来了个星核猎手，还离我们这么近，说不是故意的谁信呐。
t_en: It's waaaay too much of a coincidence. A Stellaron Hunter shows up at the site of a Stellaron burst that just happens to be occurring nearby. Does anyone really believe that?

t_ch: 气死我咧，自顾自地劫持通信，又自顾自地挂了，真没教养！{NICKNAME}，听说你以前和她很熟？这人一直是这个德性吗？
t_en: I'm so mad! She just hijacks our communications and then hangs up? So rude! {NICKNAME}, you used to know her, right? Was she always like this?

t_ch: 我不记得了。
t_en: I don't remember.

t_ch: 咱们车上有一个失忆设定的就够了啊。
t_en: ...We don't need another amnesiac on this train...

t_ch: 她以前挺好的。
t_en: She used to be nice.

t_ch: 是吗？啧，那可能她特别喜欢你吧……
t_en: Oh yeah? Huh, maybe she really liked you...

t_ch: {M#男人就该对过去守口如瓶。}{F#不能背地里议论女孩子。}
t_en: {M#A man should keep his past a secret.}{F#You shouldn't talk about girls behind their backs.}

t_ch: {M#你这个时候倒挺有担当的耶……}{F#你这个时候倒挺有担当的耶……}
t_en: Pfft, taking things seriously all of a sudden...?

t_ch: 对了，我刚刚跟丹恒打了招呼，也跟你通个气哈：咱们到时候一起投反对票！
t_en: Oh yeah, I just spoke to Dan Heng, here's the deal: We've all gotta vote against!

t_ch: 这不是姬子说了算吗？
t_en: Doesn't Himeko have the final say?

t_ch: 你信我，她肯定搞民主投票。你一票，我一票，丹恒一票，这就三票了。去哪儿还不是咱们一句话的事儿。
t_en: Trust me, she'll hold a democratic vote. One for you, one for me, one for Dan Heng — three altogether. We never go anywhere without a consensus.

t_ch: 我想投赞成票。
t_en: I want to vote in favor.

t_ch: 哼，就知道你还是想听星核猎手的。算啦，你再好好想想。
t_en: Hmph, I should've known you'd listen to that Stellaron Hunter. Whatever — but you should think before you do that.

t_ch: 反正我就是来气。
t_en: Ugh, I'm so annoyed.

t_ch: 不过在房间里耽搁了一会，就又发生意外了啊。
t_en: ...Seems like the longer I spend in my room, the stranger things are when I emerge.

t_ch: 我们可能要去仙舟「罗浮」了。
t_en: We might be going to the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 嗯，三月七告诉我了…若是别的仙舟或许还好，「罗浮」…我不能去。
t_en: Mm, March told me... *sigh* If it were one of the other ships it might've been okay, but I can't go to the Luofu.

t_ch: 你和罗浮有什么过节？
t_en: So you and the Luofu have history?

t_ch: 我被禁止踏入罗浮。「凡所治处，不得履踏」，他们是这样宣布的。
t_en: I'm forbidden from setting foot on that ship. "Total interception of any attempt to enter the dominion" is how they chose to put it. *sigh*

t_ch: …如果你们要去的话，我不能和你们同行。
t_en: ...So if you're planning on going, I can't go with you.

t_ch: 别的仙舟？那是什么？
t_en: Other ships? Which ones?

t_ch: 联盟的仙舟共有六艘，「罗浮」只是其中之一。
t_en: The Alliance is made up of six ships — the Luofu is only one of them.

t_ch: 此外还有「曜青」、「方壶」、「朱明」、「虚陵」和「玉阙」。
t_en: There are also the Yaoqing, the Fanghu, the Zhuming, the Xuling, and the Yuque.

t_ch: 好吧，这是你的自由。
t_en: Well, that's up to you.

t_ch: 在我记忆里，仙舟还是很壮丽的。祝你们顺利。
t_en: I remember the Xianzhou was a sight to behold. Good luck.

t_ch: 你还好吗，丹恒？小三月把事情都告诉你了吧？
t_en: Are you okay, Dan Heng? March brought you up to speed, I assume?

t_ch: 我没事，情况也了解了。
t_en: I'm fine, and yes, I'm up to speed.

t_ch: 嗯，那么，针对这一特别事项，我们投票决定前进的方向吧。
t_en: Good. In that case, with regard to the matter at hand, let's vote to decide our next move.

t_ch: 星核猎手声称一颗星核在仙舟「罗浮」上爆发了，而列车离它很近。如果我们前往罗浮，可能有机会拯救许多无辜者的生命。
t_en: Our Stellaron Hunter claims that a Stellaron burst has occurred on the Xianzhou Luofu, and that we're in the vicinity. If we head for the Luofu, we may have the chance to save many innocent lives.

t_ch: …也有可能，我们只是被星核猎手利用，上当受骗了而已。瓦尔特和我都认为星核猎手没说实话，但也不能完全无视她的情报……
t_en: ...However, it's also possible that our Stellaron Hunter is lying and using us for her own ends. Neither Welt nor I believe that she's telling us the truth, but we can't just ignore the intel she's given us...

t_ch: 所以，是否转向前往仙舟，我们用民主投票的方式来决议：伸出手掌代表同意，不伸手代表反对。
t_en: So, we're about to hold a democratic vote on whether to set a course for the Xianzhou. All those in favor, hold out your hand. All those against, keep your arms by your side.

t_ch: 三、二、一——
t_en: Three, two, one...

t_ch: 伸手！
t_en: Hands out!

t_ch: 四对一，那么，列车团以多数票同意前往仙舟！
t_en: Four against one. Well, it looks like the Astral Express crew has a majority vote to head for the Xianzhou!

t_ch: 不伸手！
t_en: Arms by my side!

t_ch: 三对二，那么，列车团以多数票同意前往仙舟！
t_en: Three against two. Well, it looks like the Astral Express crew has a majority vote to head for the Xianzhou!

t_ch: 我是谁我在哪我要干什么！？
t_en: Wait, what am I supposed to do!?

t_ch: 三对二，那么，列车团以多数票同意前往仙舟！
t_en: Three against two. Well, it looks like the Astral Express crew has a majority vote to head for the Xianzhou!

t_ch: 你不是说要投反对票吗？！
t_en: Didn't you say you were voting against!?

t_ch: 呃，冷静下来转念一想，万一那女人没撒谎，我们不去仙舟会有很多无辜者受害耶…现在不是任性的时候…嘻嘻。
t_en: *sigh* I calmed down and thought about it a bit. If it turns out that woman isn't lying, then a lot of innocent people are gonna get hurt... Now's the time for thinking about other people... hehe *sigh*

t_ch: 你这个深明大义的女人！
t_en: Oh, how suddenly principled of you!

t_ch: 你这个没有原则的女人！
t_en: You're so capricious!

t_ch: 好啦好啦，小三月就是这样的，我们都习惯啦。
t_en: Alright, alright, I think we're all used to March's modus operandi by now.

t_ch: 怎么说，丹恒？你要留下来吗？
t_en: So, Dan Heng, you want to stay here?

t_ch: 嗯，这次我就不去了。
t_en: Yes, I'm staying this time.

t_ch: 那这次的「开拓之旅」就交给你啦，瓦尔特？看你摩拳擦掌很久了。要把{NICKNAME}和小三月照顾好喔。
t_en: In that case... Welt. Ready for a trailblazing expedition? I know you've been itching to get out there for a while now. But make sure you take good care of {NICKNAME} and March.

t_ch: 放心吧。
t_en: ...Don't worry.

t_ch: 目标仙舟——出发喽！
t_en: Destination Xianzhou, here we come!

t_ch: 哇！这就是「仙舟」？看起来比雅利洛-Ⅵ还大！
t_en: Whoa! So that's a Xianzhou ship? It looks even bigger than Jarilo-VI!

t_ch: 对仙舟人来说，这艘船就是他们的「星球」。
t_en: For Xianzhou natives, these ships are their planets.

t_ch: 大地是文明生存与发展不可或缺的摇篮。不过，也有文明能脱离童年，驾驭星舰巡游在虚空中……仙舟联盟就是如此。
t_en: Terrestrial environments are cradles that allow civilizations to survive and then develop. Some of those civilizations progress further — constructing space-faring vessels which allow them to voyage into the unknown... The Xianzhou Alliance is one such civilization.

t_ch: 我只看过几次，但，依旧这么壮丽……
t_en: I've only glimpsed it a few times, but... it's as magnificent as I remember...

t_ch: 你到底是什么情况啊，看你一副怀念又寂寞的样子？
t_en: You okay there? What's with all the lonely nostalgia vibes?

t_ch: 这里是星穹列车，重复一遍，这里是星穹列车。我已抵达仙舟空域，向地面…也许应该说「甲板」，请求登陆许可。
t_en: This is the Astral Express, I repeat, this is the Astral Express. We have arrived in Xianzhou territory. Requesting landing permission from ground control... f—from the bridge, I mean.

t_ch: 欢迎抵达…「罗浮」空港…星槎海……
t_en: Welcome to... Luofu skyport... Starskiff Haven...

t_ch: 请耐心等待接驳…请耐心等待接驳…请耐心等待接驳……
t_en: Please await transfer... Please await transfer... Please await transfer...

t_ch: 复读机吗？
t_en: Is it stuck on repeat?

t_ch: 怎么，有回家的感觉啦？
t_en: *sigh* Now you know how I feel when you copy me.

t_ch: 听起来像自动客服的录音…
t_en: Sounds like one of those automated customer service numbers...

t_ch: …怎么了？
t_en: ...Something's not right.

t_ch: 信号还在重复，但没有任何引导停泊的迹象。
t_en: The signal is still repeating, but no one is guiding us in to dock.

t_ch: 星核猎手也许没骗我们，仙舟真的出事了……
t_en: Maybe the Stellaron Hunters were telling the truth. It seems like something really did happen to the Xianzhou...

t_ch: 一艘飞船抵达空无一人的太空港——许多恐怖电影都这么开头！
t_en: A vessel arrives at a deserted spaceport... Isn't that how, like, loads of horror movies start!?

t_ch: 别胡思乱想。
t_en: Let's not let our imaginations get the better of us.

t_ch: 请耐心等待接驳…请耐心等待接驳…请耐心等待接驳……
t_en: Please await transfer... Please await transfer... Please await transfer...

t_ch: 还是智能信号吗？
t_en: Still the automated signal?

t_ch: 是的，还在重复——啊，好了！
t_en: Yes, it's still on repeat... Ah! There we go.

t_ch: ——玉界门正在开启，我代表「仙舟联盟：罗浮」，欢迎远道而来的客人，请您按照指引，有序停靠——
t_en: ...The Jade Gate is now opening. On behalf of the Xianzhou Luofu: Welcome, guests from afar. Please proceed to dock in accordance with the guidance...

t_ch: 咦？是有工作人员出现了吗？
t_en: Huh? Is this still automated?

t_ch: 信号中断了，对面什么也没说。
t_en: The signal broke off, seems that's all there is.

t_ch: 我们走了，姬子，你在列车上也要多加小心。
t_en: We should get going. Himeko, stay vigilant back here on the Express.

t_ch: 不要紧的，这不是还有丹恒在嘛。
t_en: Relax, I've got Dan Heng here with me.

t_ch: 你又不去啊？
t_en: You're staying too?

t_ch: 我可没有偷懒啊。列车上有很多事情要处理，忙得很。
t_en: Yes, but I won't be putting my feet up. There's a lot to take care of here on the train.

t_ch: 我不想去了…
t_en: I don't wanna go...

t_ch: 你少来，一下车你比谁都精神！
t_en: Come onnnn, you were the most excited last time we got off the train!

t_ch: 开拓者！出发！
t_en: Trailblazers! Assemble~!

t_ch: 走之前记得做好整备工作噢。
t_en: Make sure you're properly prepared before you set off.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，小三月，一路顺风！
t_en: Have a safe trip you guys!

t_ch: 帕姆也一起去吧？
t_en: Why not come with us, Pom-Pom?

t_ch: 帕姆很忙。列车的工作不能落下。
t_en: Pom-Pom's too busy — looking after the Express is a full-time job.

t_ch: 帕姆去过仙舟吗？
t_en: Have you visited the Xianzhou before, Pom-Pom?

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: Never.

t_ch: 你们能享受旅程，帕姆很开心。
t_en: ...Don't worry about me — I'm just happy you're enjoying the journey.

t_ch: （它真的开心吗……）
t_en: (Pom-Pom still seems a little sad...)

t_ch: 这回你们可以见识到「杨叔」的身手了。哈哈，他和你们一起行动，我很放心。
t_en: This time you'll get the chance to witness Welt in action, haha. I'm glad he's going with you.

t_ch: 看不出来啊。
t_en: So Welt's got skills?

t_ch: 他很少和人讲起自己的故事呢，你问他的时候，他只会说自己是做动画的。
t_en: He doesn't talk much about his past. If you ask him, he'll just tell you about how he worked in animation...

t_ch: 其实远远没那么简单，他的经历可比我要精彩哦。
t_en: ...But that's only a small part of his story. Believe me when I say he has more experience than I do.

t_ch: 我就知道他没那么简单。
t_en: I knew there was more to him than meets the eye...

t_ch: 所谓真人不露相吧，你问他的时候，他只会说自己是做动画的。
t_en: You could say he keeps his cards close to his chest. If you ask him, he'll just tell you about how he worked in animation...

t_ch: 其实远远没那么简单，他的经历可比我要精彩哦。
t_en: ...But that's only a small part of his story. Believe me when I say he has more experience than I do.

t_ch: 放心，我会保护好他和三月的。
t_en: Don't worry, I'll take care of March and him.

t_ch: 哈哈，那就拜托你了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Haha, much appreciated, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 宇宙这么大，偏偏遇上丹恒待过的那艘仙舟，这一定是命运！这次去仙舟，咱们趁机打听打听丹恒的事吧？
t_en: Allll this universe, and we just happen to bump into a Xianzhou ship that Dan Heng spent time on? That's gotta be fate! I wonder what Dan Heng stories await us.

t_ch: 这家伙看着就有神秘的过去，这回又恰巧让咱们撞上了罗浮，可不得一探究竟！
t_en: That guy sure has a mysterious past. We gotta take this opportunity on the Luofu to dig into it!

t_ch: 那必须的啊。
t_en: Darn straight.

t_ch: 我就知道，在八卦这方面，没人比得上你！
t_en: I knew you'd be excited! Check out the master of gossip over here.

t_ch: （她似乎完全忘了是下去执行任务的。）
t_en: (I wonder if she even remembers we've got a job to do down there...)

t_ch: 是他的隐私耶，不好吧。
t_en: Give the man some privacy, please.

t_ch: 好吧，听你的。罢了罢了，这次咱俩多拍些照片，让他狠狠羡慕羡慕。
t_en: *sigh* Fine, you're right... In that case, we'll just have to take loads of selfies till he gets super jealous.

t_ch: （她似乎完全忘了是下去执行任务的。）
t_en: (I wonder if she even remembers we've got a job to do down there...)

t_ch: 可别给我们惹出什么麻烦…
t_en: That might be dangerous...

t_ch: 不是吧，他的过去这么黑暗吗？
t_en: Huh? Is his past really that dark?

t_ch: 那算了。不管他了，这次咱俩多拍些照片，让他狠狠羡慕羡慕。
t_en: *sigh* Fine, I'll leave him alone then. We'll just have to take loads of selfies till he gets super jealous.

t_ch: （她似乎完全忘了是下去执行任务的。）
t_en: (I wonder if she even remembers we've got a job to do down there...)

t_ch: 此去仙舟，多多小心。我整理了一些仙舟情报，希望有所助益。
t_en: You need to take extra care on this expedition. I've compiled some intel on the Xianzhou for you, I hope it comes in useful.

t_ch: 在出发前，我要先同两位明确此行的目的——
t_en: Before we set off, I need to clarify the aim of this journey with you...

t_ch: 虽然星核猎手抛出了许多烟雾弹，但这次仙舟之行最重要的是……
t_en: The Stellaron Hunters have given us a lot to consider, and a lot to doubt... But the most important part of this expedition is—

t_ch: 品尝当地美食。
t_en: Sampling the local delicacies.

t_ch: 别光顾着吃啊，应该多拍些漂亮的风景。
t_en: Always thinking with your stomach. What about all the scenic photo opportunities?

t_ch: 和当权者搞好关系。
t_en: Getting to know the people with power.

t_ch: 看来贝洛伯格之行真的改变了你很多啊……
t_en: That Belobog expedition got in your head, huh...

t_ch: 星核。
t_en: The Stellaron.

t_ch: 是的。
t_en: Precisely.

t_ch: 星核猎手一定别有所图，但考虑到他们的名声，和卡芙卡言语的侧重：仙舟里有一颗星核应是不会错的。
t_en: The Stellaron Hunters clearly have ulterior motives. However, knowing what they're all about and given the focus of Kafka's words, I have no doubt that the Xianzhou is dealing with a Stellaron.

t_ch: 联盟和列车过去没什么来往，我们的到来并不一定受到欢迎。但开拓者并不图名利，我们要为仙舟提供力所能及的帮助，尽可能地消弭祸端。
t_en: The Alliance and the Express haven't had any previous dealings, so our arrival might not receive the warmest welcome. But as Trailblazers, we're not in this for fame or gain — we just need to do everything in our power to assist the Xianzhou and eliminate the source of disaster.

t_ch: 保持住这一本心，贯彻「探索」、「了解」、「建立」、「连结」的开拓之道吧。
t_en: Always keep that in mind, and don't forget the way of the trailblaze: Explore, understand, establish, and connect.

t_ch: 噢噢噢！
t_en: Yeahhhh~!

t_ch: 出发！
t_en: ...Let's go!

t_ch: 再等一等，会议就开始了。
t_en: The meeting will start soon.

t_ch: 房间里静悄悄，没有回应。
t_en: The room is quiet. Nobody answers.

t_ch: （丹恒看起来心事重重的样子，还是先给他一点独处的时间让他静一静吧。）
t_en: (Dan Heng seems perturbed, maybe I should give him some alone time to calm down.)

t_ch: 瞧，跟我说的一样：要是某一天你醒来没见到丹恒，那多半一天都见不到他。
t_en: See, it's like I said: If you wake up and Dan Heng's not around, then you probably won't see him the rest of the day either.

t_ch: 错过会议不要紧吗？
t_en: Didn't Himeko say not to worry?

t_ch: 不知道耶。老实讲，航线会议的性质大概就跟你常挂在嘴边的团建是一回事吧。参不参加都无所谓，当然姬子姐总是希望大家都来的。
t_en: I guess. To be honest, warp navigation meetings are basically just like that "teambuilding" you're always going on about. You don't have to show up, but Himeko might be upset if you don't.

t_ch: 你想啊，管理资料室的是丹恒，下一个目的地的情况他一清二楚。然后这信息姬子姐和杨叔也知道——那不就瞒着我和你两个人嘛！
t_en: Think about it, Dan Heng's the one managing the archives — he'll already be clued in to our next destination. Himeko and Welt know already, of course... so it's just me and you in the dark!

t_ch: 丹恒好神秘啊。
t_en: Dan Heng sure is a mysterious guy.

t_ch: 嗯…听说他以前干过很了不得的事情，现在还被某个厉害势力通缉在册。我猜是仙舟联盟，流光忆庭也有可能。可以排除公司，公司的通缉名单我见过。
t_en: Yeah... I heard he did something incredible once, and now some powerful force has his name on a wanted list. My guess is the Xianzhou Alliance or the Garden of Recollection. In any case, it can't be the IPC — I've seen their wanted list.

t_ch: 唔…多半是流光忆庭了。你没发现他特别热衷和黑塔打交道吗？黑塔和流光忆庭那是出了名的不对付，所谓敌人的敌人就是朋友……
t_en: Hmm... I bet it is the Garden of Recollection. Didn't it seem like Dan Heng was super keen on talking to Herta? Everyone knows that Herta and the Garden of Recollection don't get along. They say the enemy of your enemy is your friend...

t_ch: 真是意外。
t_en: How shocking...

t_ch: 哈！这才哪到哪啊。要我说你是初来乍到，等你待久了，会发现列车上每个人都怪怪的……
t_en: Ha! Shocking? I guess you're still new — you'll find out how strange everyone is around here sooner or later...

t_ch: 别这样看着我，难道我不怪吗？我可是把过去都忘光了，你见过哪个失忆的没点神奇的背景故事？我给自己都编了几十个啦。
t_en: What's with the funny look? Am I not strange enough for you? Name me an amnesiac that didn't have some crazy backstory. I already made up a few dozen for myself.

t_ch: 气色不错，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: You're looking well, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生这次会一起来吗？
t_en: You coming with us this time?

t_ch: 哈哈，当然了。看你们在雅利洛-Ⅵ大展拳脚，我也跃跃欲试哪。
t_en: Haha, you better believe it. You guys really put on a show for Jarilo-VI — I'm eager to join in myself.

t_ch: 不瞒你说，好几次我都想去助你们一臂之力；但是姬子说，要让孩子们自己体会一次开拓的不易……
t_en: I gotta say, there were more than a few times where I wanted to lend a hand... but Himeko said I should let you youngsters rise to the trailblazing challenges...

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生知道目的地吧？
t_en: Do you know what our next destination is?

t_ch: 嗯，我知道，但还不能说。姬子希望给你和三月七保留一点神秘感。
t_en: I know alright, but I can't tell you yet. Himeko wants to create a sense of mystery for you and March.

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生希望去什么世界？
t_en: What world do you want to go to?

t_ch: 想去的世界嘛？…是故乡吧。但很遗憾，在列车星图未损坏的记录里，没有它的位置。
t_en: That I want to go to? ...My home world, I suppose. Sadly, its location doesn't appear on the Express' astral chart records, at least the ones that are undamaged.

t_ch: 我想在回家之前多看看其他的文明，也算是不浪费搭乘星穹列车的这段时光。
t_en: In any case, I do want to see some other civilizations before I go home. It'd be a crime to waste this opportunity on the Express.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，停靠站点的介绍拿到了吗？期待吗？
t_en: Ahem... {NICKNAME}, did you receive the destination overview? Excited?

t_ch: 姬子要在会议上宣布。
t_en: Himeko's going to announce it in the meeting.

t_ch: 原来你还不知道啊……
t_en: So you don't know yet...

t_ch: （说谎）拿到了。
t_en: We got it.

t_ch: 完全没看出期待的样子……
t_en: You don't seem very excited...

t_ch: 经历了雅利洛-Ⅵ上冰冷、疲惫的旅程，难道不期待下一站是个怎样的星球吗！
t_en: After all that cold and exhaustion on Jarilo-VI, you must be looking forward to the next planet!

t_ch: 什么样的星球都喜欢！
t_en: I like all planets equally!

t_ch: 帕姆很高兴你能享受这趟旅行！
t_en: Pom-Pom's thrilled that you're enjoying your journey!

t_ch: 很久很久以前，这辆列车正是这样将无名客带往一个又一个的星球…现在它又像过去一样了。
t_en: A long, long time ago, this train would ferry the Nameless from planet to planet... Now it's able to do the same again.

t_ch: 什么时候能抵达裴迦纳呢？
t_en: When will we get to Pegana?

t_ch: 裴迦纳吗？它在航线的终点哦，那是阿基维利的故乡……帕姆也想要回去那里，可惜旅途和人生一样，没法一蹴而就帕。
t_en: Pegana? That's the final stop — Akivili's home world... Pom-Pom wants to visit again too. Unfortunately, the journey's a lot like life — you don't always get where you're going right away.

t_ch: 千年过去，这些介绍还有用吗？
t_en: Are these destination overviews still useful after a thousand years?

t_ch: …总有些星球没被星核污染啦。
t_en: ...There are some planets that haven't been affected by Stellarons.

t_ch: （你从帕姆耳朵的弧度上读出了一丝伤感。）
t_en: (You can tell Pom-Pom's a little sad by looking at their ears.)

t_ch: 列车的速度很快，但它开得再快也没法赶上变化帕……
t_en: The Express moves at an incredible speed, but change is always faster...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，准备好的话，就到车头附近集合吧，会议要开始了！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, if you're ready, head over to the front of the train. The meeting is about to start!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，准备好的话，就到车头附近集合吧，会议要开始了！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, if you're ready, head over to the front of the train. The meeting is about to start!

t_ch: 各位乘客！跃迁航线会议正式开始！
t_en: Passengers! The warp navigation meeting has officially begun!

t_ch: 首先，帕姆要感谢各位无名客成功解决了本站点的问题，让列车能继续在星轨上奔驰！
t_en: Firstly, Pom-Pom wants to congratulate you, the Nameless, for successfully resolving the problems at this stop. The Express is now able to continue along the star rail!

t_ch: 这可是我们三个的功劳哦，对吧{NICKNAME}！
t_en: The three of us actually pulled it off, huh {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 接下来，本列车长要宣布下一站的名字——
t_en: It's time for your conductor — ahem, that's me — to reveal the name of our next stop...

t_ch: 暂时无法离开当前场景，需要完成开拓任务「星间流浪」
t_en: Unable to leave the current area, please complete Trailblaze Mission "Drifting Between the Stars"

t_ch: 暂时无法离开当前场景，需要完成开拓任务「激『冻』人心的大冒险」
t_en: Unable to leave the current area, please complete Trailblaze Mission "A Grand Cool Adventure"

t_ch: 暂时无法离开当前场景，需要完成开拓任务「静静的星河」
t_en: Unable to leave the current area, please complete Trailblaze Mission "Silent Galaxy"

t_ch: 暂时无法离开当前场景，需要完成开拓任务「旅进青霄，不速之邀」
t_en: Unable to leave the current area, please complete Trailblaze Mission "An Invitation Without Proffer"

t_ch: 暂时无法离开当前场景，需要完成冒险任务「流光忆彩」
t_en: Unable to leave the current area, please complete Adventure Mission "Fleeting Lights"

t_ch: 到家！列车停靠了这么久，姬子和杨叔估计都不耐烦了吧？
t_en: Home, sweet home! Mr. Yang and Himeko must be bored given how long our train had to stay here.

t_ch: 下个目的地会是哪里？咱都等不及了，好期待呀！
t_en: Where's our next stop? I can't wait!

t_ch: 欢迎回家，三位。
t_en: Welcome home, you three.

t_ch: 姬子，杨叔！开拓小分队的第一次任务，这就顺利完成啦！
t_en: Himeko, Mr. Yang! Team Trailblaze, back from duty! Mission one: Accomplished!

t_ch: 虽然我俩没有亲身参与这次「开拓之旅」，但光从结果就能看出大家有多努力。恭喜各位——尤其是{NICKNAME}——大家对你的首秀评价很高。
t_en: *chuckles* Although Welt and I didn't get the chance to go with you, I can tell you worked hard to bring things back to normal. Well done, everybody. And especially you, {NICKNAME}. I heard a lot of good things about you.

t_ch: 所以，我的任务评分是…
t_en: How would you rate my performance?

t_ch: 假如真有这么一套评分系统，我一定会给你最高分。
t_en: I'd say it's a solid five stars.

t_ch: 小菜一碟。
t_en: Piece of cake.

t_ch: 自信是你最强有力的武器。
t_en: Confidence goes a long way.

t_ch: 那我下次能申请休息吗？
t_en: Can I skip the next mission and get some rest?

t_ch: 然后错过一趟精彩的大冒险？我不确定你会喜欢那种感觉。
t_en: *chuckles* And miss out on a great adventure? I don't think you'd like that.

t_ch: 好好在列车上休息一阵吧，缓解一下在雅利洛-Ⅵ上累积的疲劳感。
t_en: Get some rest on the Express. Jarilo-VI was not an easy ride.

t_ch: 帕姆认为我们应该尽早驶离这片间域，避免刚刚有所缓解的空间异常现象出现反复。我和姬子都同意列车长的观点。
t_en: Pom-Pom is of the opinion that we should leave as soon as possible to avoid potential resurgences of the anomalies we experienced earlier. Himeko and I agree.

t_ch: 也就是说，我们要尽快决定接下来的目的地了。
t_en: ...Which means we need to decide where our next destination is, soon.

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: That's right.

t_ch: 这就是无名客的命途吧？永远在路上，永远不停下「开拓」的脚步……
t_en: That's the path of the Nameless, I presume? Always on the road, always trailblazing...

t_ch: 哎，我可没在抱怨啊！惊喜一个接着一个，这样的旅行节奏正合咱的胃口！
t_en: I—I'm not complaining by the way! One adventure after another, that's exactly what I signed up for!

t_ch: 看来小三月已经喜欢上无名客的生活方式了，真好。
t_en: Looks like March has fallen in love with the way of the Nameless. That's good to hear.

t_ch: 各位可以先回自己的房间休息，或者在这节车厢稍歇，喝杯咖啡之类的。大家都是熟人了，随意便好。
t_en: You can head back to your rooms for some rest, or stay here and make yourselves a cup of coffee, whichever you like.

t_ch: 那我先回房间了。
t_en: I'll head back to my room then.

t_ch: 唉…每次我都以为，那家伙能变得更合群些……
t_en: *sigh* There I was thinking this guy would gradually open up...

t_ch: 有些事不必强求，三月。我觉得那孩子很珍惜跟你们同行的时光。
t_en: *chuckles* We need to give him some time, March. I think he treasures the time spent with you guys.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}——关于和「大守护者」的最后一战、还有「星核」的情况，我猜你心底还藏着不少疑问吧？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, about your battle against the Supreme Guardian and the whole Stellaron situation, you must have a lot of unanswered questions, I suppose?

t_ch: 趁着你们不在的时候，我和瓦尔特先生做了些观测，总结出了些结果。如果你需要解惑的话，不妨跟我们聊聊。
t_en: While you were away, Welt and I did some research and came to some interesting conclusions. Let me know if you want to ask about anything.

t_ch: 列车行走在「开拓」的命途之上，走过一个个奇妙的世界，邂逅一群群鲜活的生命，见证一段段美丽的故事。
t_en: The Express crew tread upon the Trailblaze. We travel between worlds, like a thread weaved into the life stories of others.

t_ch: 宇宙浩瀚无边——我们能穿越星海擦肩而过，本已是奇迹般的缘分。若有机会能聊上几句、认识彼此，甚至结伴而行…这美妙的概率或许只能用「命运」解释。
t_en: The universe is vast beyond our imagination. It's a miracle that our paths converge. The fact that we get to know and travel with each other can only be seen as the design of destiny.

t_ch: 谢谢你选择了成为我们的伙伴，{NICKNAME}。希望我能解答你在旅途中萌生的疑惑。
t_en: Thank you for becoming our companion, {NICKNAME}. I'll do my best to answer any questions you may have.

t_ch: 我想聊聊和可可利亚的决战。
t_en: I'd like to talk about the battle with Cocolia.

t_ch: 很乐意。所谓当局者迷——也许我能从旁观者的角度帮你找到答案。
t_en: Sure. Oftentimes those who are involved in historic events don't have the opportunity to see things from a wider perspective. I hope as an onlooker I may provide some help.

t_ch: 关于「存护」的命途…
t_en: About the Path of Preservation...

t_ch: 「存护」克里珀是一位淡漠的星神。除非你能始终保持「公司」那种非同一般的狂热…否则恐怕很难分到祂的力量。
t_en: Qlipoth the Preservation is known for THEIR... indifference. Unless you become a zealot like those with the Corporation, it's hard to share in the Aeon's power.

t_ch: 借着强烈的「存护」执念，贝洛伯格的筑城者们坚持了数百年的时间。但随着裂界的腐蚀逐渐深入，本就脆弱的信仰也出现了裂痕。
t_en: Determined to preserve the world they love, the Architects held on for hundreds of years. But as the Fragmentum continued to advance, their already wavering faith started to show some cracks.

t_ch: 那位大守护者，可可利亚——她恐怕彻底放弃了践行「存护」的命途。和她相反，你在那场战斗中展现出了坚定不移的「存护」意志。
t_en: And one particular guardian, Cocolia, abandoned the Path of Preservation altogether. In contrast, you stood your ground and proved your faith in the Amber Lord.

t_ch: 从结果上看，克里珀回应了你强烈的愿望。你能够挥舞大守护者的武器、并操纵那尊巨像，大概也是由于祂那一瞥吧？
t_en: Judging by how the battle turned out, Qlipoth responded to your wish. THEIR gaze infused you with the power to wield the weapon of a supreme guardian and to take control of the Colossus.

t_ch: 我想谢谢你提供的支援。
t_en: I was meant to thank you for your support during the battle.

t_ch: 小事。虽然我和瓦尔特先生没有参与这次「开拓」，但你们在雅利洛-Ⅵ上的一举一动，我们全都看在眼里。
t_en: *chuckles* Don't mention it. Welt and I may have stayed on the Express this whole time, but we've been watching over you the moment you set foot on Jarilo-VI.

t_ch: 同伴就该相互照应，我们「无名客」更是如此。也请时刻记得，哪怕肉身相隔千万里，列车永远是你最坚实的后盾。
t_en: Friends look after each other, the same goes for us Nameless. Please remember, no matter where you are in the universe, the Express will always be behind you.

t_ch: 可可利亚的力量来源…
t_en: The source of Cocolia's power...

t_ch: 毫无疑问，是「星核」。
t_en: ...is a Stellaron, there's no doubt about that.

t_ch: 结合我的分析和猜测，那位大守护者恐怕在早年就遭受了「星核」的精神侵蚀——此后发生的一切悲剧都滥觞于此。
t_en: What I've gathered so far suggests it's likely that the supreme guardian was corrupted by the Stellaron at a very young age. All the tragedies that came after can be traced to that point...

t_ch: 可恨之人尚有可怜之处。如果换成你我坐在她的位置上，每天面对逐渐衰落的城市…我们真的能紧握脆弱的信仰，没有丝毫动摇吗？
t_en: On the flip side, we can't judge Cocolia too harshly. Imagine if it were you or me who had to sit on the throne and watch the city slowly being brought to its knees... Would we not question what we believed in?

t_ch: 我们聊些别的吧。
t_en: Let's talk about something else.

t_ch: 当然可以。我们不缺时间。
t_en: Sure, ask away. We've got plenty of time.

t_ch: 雅利洛-Ⅵ还能回到以前吗？
t_en: Can Jarilo-VI go back to what it was like before the Stellaron?

t_ch: 这个问题，也许瓦尔特先生能给你更详实的分析解答。不过，既然你想知道我的看法……
t_en: Welt may be better equipped to answer that. But if you want my two cents...

t_ch: …我觉得，很难。并非没有可能，但过程无疑会十分艰辛。
t_en: I'd say it's unlikely. I guess it's possible, but it would take a long time.

t_ch: 从你们的报告里我能看出，贝洛伯格的新任大守护者是一位英明、聪颖的年轻人。更加难能可贵的是，她还在这段旅程中抛弃了不必要的天真。
t_en: I can tell from your report that the new Supreme Guardian of Belobog is a smart young lady with leadership qualities. What's more important is that she now understands her people after the journey with you.

t_ch: 在她的身边还有一群坚定、正直的支持者，这是大幸。可命运难以捉摸，时常荒谬——就算在他们的有生之年里，雅利洛-Ⅵ能够恢复一定程度的繁荣……
t_en: The firm and honest support she gets from people around her will also prove immensely useful. But fate likes to play tricks on people. Even if during their lifetime they managed to make Jarilo-VI prosper again to a certain degree...

t_ch: 但对于潜伏在宇宙中的无数威胁而言，他们依旧脆弱、无助。作为见证者，我们只能在远处为他们祈福。
t_en: They will remain vulnerable and helpless to countless threats lurking in the recesses of the universe. We can only give them our best wishes from afar.

t_ch: 「星核」怎么样了？
t_en: What happened to the Stellaron?

t_ch: 想摧毁「星核」几乎是不可能的任务。连黑塔也没能找到彻底破坏「星核」结构的方法，只能创造条件抑制它的能量漫溢。
t_en: It's virtually impossible to destroy a Stellaron. No one, not even Herta, has found a way to break its structure. The best we can do at this stage is to prevent its energy from overflowing.

t_ch: 大守护者战败之际，向其导送能量的「星核」也露出了一丝破绽，让我和瓦尔特找到了压制它的时机。
t_en: When the supreme guardian was defeated, the Stellaron that had been transmitting energy to her exposed its weakness to us for a brief period of time, allowing Welt and I to contain its power.

t_ch: 列车能对「星核」做的事也仅限于「抑制」而已，这种做法只能解一时之需…
t_en: That's about all we can do about a Stellaron. It may be suppressed for now, but it's only a matter of time before it regains power.

t_ch: …不过，倒是足够让这个星球上的人们喘口气了。
t_en: But this brief window should allow the people on this planet to heal and rebuild.

t_ch: 谢谢你的耐心，姬子。
t_en: Thanks for your patience, Himeko.

t_ch: 不用客气，{NICKNAME}。好好休息吧，这是你应得的奖赏。
t_en: You're most welcome, {NICKNAME}. Have some rest, you've earned it.

t_ch: 你的首次「开拓之旅」顺利结束——感觉如何，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: So, your first trailblazing expedition has concluded. How do you feel, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 欢欣或痛苦，圆满或遗憾，都是旅途的一体两面。只要行走在「开拓」的命途上，这些感悟就会一路相随，直到成为习惯。
t_en: Be it joy or pain, success or regret, it's all part of the journey. Those feelings will accompany you as long as you tread on the Trailblaze, and one day you'll grow used to them.

t_ch: 你似乎有话想问。尽管开口，我也正想找人谈谈心…健谈如姬子，也快跟我把话题耗尽了。
t_en: It seems you have something on your mind. Let's hear it. I'd like to hear a fresh perspective myself... Hehe, as talkative as Himeko is, we've pretty much run out of topics.

t_ch: 裂界和寒潮…会消失吗？
t_en: The Fragmentum and the Eternal Freeze... will they disappear?

t_ch: 恐怕不会。
t_en: I'm afraid not.

t_ch: 如我之前所说，「星核」给雅利洛-Ⅵ造成的影响已经扎根太深。在可预见的未来，贝洛伯格的人们仍将继续与灾祸搏斗。
t_en: As I've mentioned before, the Stellaron's influence on Jarilo-VI is more profound than we might think. I don't think Belobog's fight against the lingering effects of the Stellaron will end any time soon.

t_ch: 但，胜利的天平已经在向人类倾斜。究其原因，是因为你们放上了名为「希望」的砝码。
t_en: But, the balance of power is tilting ever so slightly towards humanity, the crucial factor being the hope you brought about.

t_ch: 不要低估「希望」。它虽无色无形、不可触及，却蕴含着无限的能量。同为可被播下的种子，在我看来，它远比「星核」更为强大。
t_en: As intangible as it may sound, don't underestimate hope because the power it inspires has no limit. Like the Stellaron, it's a seed that may grow into something much bigger. But in my opinion, the fruit that hope bears will have a much bigger impact than that of the Stellaron.

t_ch: 我把你的话告诉了布洛妮娅。
t_en: I relayed your view of the Stellaron's effects to Bronya.

t_ch: 原来如此——我觉得你做了正确的事。背负着知识的重量砥砺前行，总好过在无知中盲目乐观地活着。
t_en: Is that so? Well, I think you did the right thing. It's better to move forward with the burden of knowledge than to tread a perilous road with blind optimism.

t_ch: 我依稀记得这样一个世界，那里的人们每时每刻都面临着远超常识认知的威胁。但凭借智慧和勇气，人类一次又一次地从危机中拯救了自己的家园。
t_en: I remember a distant world where the people were faced with unimaginable danger every single day. But they saved their planet time after time with wisdom and courage.

t_ch: 这就是平凡且伟大的赞歌。但愿雅利洛-Ⅵ的人们也能从自己的经历中参会同样的精神。
t_en: This goes to show how much of a difference ordinary people can make. I hope Jarilo-VI sees that too.

t_ch: 有你在的话，旅程会轻松许多。
t_en: This mission would've been much easier if you were there.

t_ch: 我原本也希望能和你们共同前往，但姬子坚持要给各位独处的时间。
t_en: Hehe... I, too, wished to go with you. But Himeko insisted on letting you and your friends rise to the challenge.

t_ch: 我一直在列车上观测你们三人的行动，期间也不少次手心捏汗。但你们总能交到值得信任的朋友，化解旅途中的困难——仅这一点，我也不得不佩服。
t_en: I'd been observing what's happening on the ground from the Express, and I have to admit that I got sweaty palms a couple of times. I was glad to see you three meet friends you could trust and overcome hardships. Well done.

t_ch: 姬子和我不同，她从始至终都表现得十分冷静，还不断劝我要相信年轻人的决断。
t_en: Himeko was calm from the very beginning. She kept telling me to trust you youngsters' decisions.

t_ch: 果然，和她相比，我还是少了些放手的魄力。
t_en: Hehe... And she was right. I really need to stop worrying so much.

t_ch: 就先聊到这儿吧，杨叔。
t_en: It's been nice chatting with you, Mr. Yang.

t_ch: 和你聊天总能有所收获。注意休息，旅程很快就要重启了。
t_en: Likewise. Get some sleep, we'll be heading off again soon.

t_ch: 帕姆，我们回来啦！
t_en: Pom-Pom, we're back!

t_ch: …嘘！正在进行星轨稳定率检测，请别打岔！
t_en: ...Shhh! Checking star rail stability, Pom-Pom needs to focus!

t_ch: 什么呀，一点都不热情……
t_en: Good to see you too...

t_ch: 空间读数…恢复到正常区间！
t_en: Spatial readout... back to the normal bracket!

t_ch: 星轨稳定率，<unbreak>56%</unbreak>…<unbreak>57%</unbreak>…持续回升中！
t_en: Star rail stability keeps rising... <unbreak>56%</unbreak>, <unbreak>57%</unbreak>...

t_ch: 行驶环境已达到稳定阈值！允许列车启动！
t_en: The numbers have stabilized! Dispatch the Express!

t_ch: …哦，欢迎{NICKNAME}和三月乘客回到列车——「开拓任务」顺利完成，列车组感谢你们做出的贡献！
t_en: ...Oh, welcome back to the Express. You've accomplished your Trailblaze Mission, the Express appreciates your help!

t_ch: 列车的状态如何？
t_en: How's the Express doing?

t_ch: 托你们的福，空间崩裂现象已经逐渐复原，星轨的稳定程度也达到了合格标准…
t_en: Thanks to you, the Space Fissure is healing up, and the star rail has become stable enough for us to dispatch the Express...

t_ch: 至于列车本身，帕姆已经做了从头到尾的细节检查，一切设备正常！
t_en: As for the coaches, Pom-Pom's checked every nook and cranny and made sure everything is in good order!

t_ch: 坏了，忘带纪念品回来了。
t_en: Oops, I forgot to bring you souvenirs.

t_ch: …算啦！反正雅利洛-Ⅵ上大概也没有什么稀奇的特产。再加上你是第一次执行开拓任务，这次就原谅你啦！
t_en: ...Don't worry about it! Jarilo-VI probably doesn't have anything worth buying anyway. Plus, it's your first time on a Trailblaze Mission, so Pom-Pom will let it slide this time!

t_ch: 不过，下次你可就没有理由了帕！
t_en: But don't forget next time!

t_ch: 下个目的地是哪儿？
t_en: Where's our next stop?

t_ch: 停靠站点要等到航线会议再公布。
t_en: The destination will be announced at the navigation meeting.

t_ch: 保持激动的好奇心好好等着帕！
t_en: For now, get some rest and stay tuned!

t_ch: 三月七的果汁…
t_en: Need to get some juice for March.

t_ch: 哦，帕姆想起来了！
t_en: Oh, Pom-Pom almost forgot!

t_ch: 喏——拿好了，别洒到地板上！
t_en: There you go, careful not to spill it!

t_ch: 谢谢列车长！
t_en: Thank you, conductor!

t_ch: 列车长，什么时候发车？
t_en: When are we leaving, conductor?

t_ch: 马上！
t_en: Right away!

t_ch: 为防止空间异常现象出现反复，列车将尽快离开当前站点，驶入平稳间域。
t_en: To avoid encountering the anomalies again, the Express will dispatch shortly and head to a more stable area.

t_ch: 安全起见，建议各位乘客扶稳坐好，待列车进入匀速行驶阶段后再四处走动！
t_en: For your safety, please don't leave your seat until the Express has reached cruising speed!

t_ch: 喂——喂喂——
t_en: Hello? Hello hello?

t_ch: 久等啦，各位乘客！感谢三位开拓者的鼎力协助，裂界活性已经降到最低，星轨各项读数已经恢复至正常区间！
t_en: It's about time we hit the road, passengers! Thanks to the three trailblazers, the activity of the Fragmentum has reached the lowest level, and the readings regarding the status of the star rail have returned to normal!

t_ch: 列车即将重新启程，驶离雅利洛-Ⅵ。请大家坐稳扶好，最后再和这颗星球说声再见吧！
t_en: Pom-Pom will dispatch the Express shortly and we'll be leaving Jarilo-VI. Please be seated and say your goodbyes to this planet!

t_ch: 欢迎来到「存护」庇佑的贝洛伯格，朋友！愿你的未来像克里珀的琥珀一样金黄剔透！
t_en: My friend! Welcome to Belobog, a city protected by Preservation. May your future be as bright and golden as Qlipoth's Amber!

t_ch: 看看你周围吧，这里是可是行政区，永冬之城繁华又美丽的心脏！
t_en: Look around you. This is the administrative district, the bustling heart of the Everwinter City!

t_ch: 在这「存护」之力编织而成的清澈蓝天之下，你我早已忘记那风雪和寒冷，向阳而生！
t_en: Under the clear blue sky made possible by Preservation, you and I have long forgotten the wind and chill of winter. We live for the sun!

t_ch: 赞美筑城者，赞美贝洛伯格——加入我吧，让我们一起为大守护者献上礼赞的歌声！
t_en: Praise the Architects of this city, and praise Belobog! Join me and let's sing a song of praise for the Supreme Guardian!

t_ch: 希望你们的探索有所进展，{NICKNAME}，也希望病痛远离你们的旅程。
t_en: I hope your exploration will make progress, {NICKNAME}, and your journey will be free of pain.

t_ch: 当然，就算不小心受了伤也不用慌张，全都交给医生就好了。
t_en: Of course, even if you get injured by accident, no need to panic. Just leave it to the doctor.

t_ch: 好羡慕你们喔，帕姆现在还不能离开这辆列车……
t_en: Pom-Pom is so jealous of you guys. Pom-Pom still can't leave the train yet...

t_ch: 累了，想歇息一会儿。
t_en: I'm tired. I need some rest.

t_ch: 列车有什么供应品吗？
t_en: What supplies are available on the Express?

t_ch: 我想使用合成机。
t_en: I want to use the Synthesizer.

t_ch: 我的开拓等级如何了？
t_en: How's my Trailblaze Level?

t_ch: 我要走啦。
t_en: I'm heading out.

t_ch: 这是太空电梯，你乘坐它来到空间站。至于你如何用言灵术控制电梯保安的故事，这里不再赘述。
t_en: This is the space elevator, which you took to arrive at the space station. As for how you used the enchantment of Spirit Whisper on the elevator security staff — there's no need to go over that right now.

t_ch: 太空电梯，它对你已经没用了。
t_en: Space elevator? You've no use for that.

t_ch: 空间站的终端，你用不着它。
t_en: You don't need any space station terminal.

t_ch: 桌子上散落着写满算式的纸，它的主人离开得很匆忙。
t_en: Papers covered in math lie scattered on the table. Their author seems to have left in a hurry.

t_ch: 终端上显示「紧急避难」，负责接待的前台服从了指示。
t_en: "Emergency Evacuation" flashed onto the display, and the front-of-house receptionist carried out the instructions.

t_ch: 这就是「星核」——你们星核猎手在宇宙中四处猎捕的东西。不过，它只是个幌子。艾利欧知道，刃知道，你也知道。星核猎手还有更重要的目标。
t_en: This is Stellaron — what Stellaron Hunters throughout the universe all hunt for. But in truth, it's all just a front. Elio knows it, Blade knows it, and you know it, too. Stellaron Hunters have another true goal.

t_ch: 为此，这颗被黑塔捕获并封印的星核，将别有它用。当银狼解开封印时，你就要拿走它，把它放进早已为此刻准备多时的「容器」里……
t_en: That's why the Stellaron that Herta sealed away will be put to another use. Once Silver Wolf breaks its seal, you'll have to take it and place it inside a kind of "vessel" that's been waiting for just this occasion...

t_ch: 这就是「星核」——某位星神把它散播到银河各处，制造混乱和灾祸。
t_en: This is Stellaron — spread throughout the cosmos by a wandering Aeon, sowing chaos and disaster.

t_ch: 这是黑塔女士，或各位更熟知的称号：天才俱乐部#83。她年少时即解开孤波算法难题、斯帕克模型猜想；青年时发现了西格玛重子的转化方法；中年时提出黑塔序列，发表关于返老还童的论文。
t_en: This is Madam Herta, or better known as member <unbreak>83</unbreak> of the Genius Society. In her childhood, she solved the solitary waves theory and the Spark model hypothesis. In her adolescence, she found the Sigma Baryons conversion method. In middle-age, she proposed the Herta sequence and published a paper on de-aging.

t_ch: 老年时，她返老还童；年少时再度解开虚数流溢现象的奥秘，捕捉天外星核并将之封印；黑塔女士曾十九次将所在星球从毁灭的危机中拯救，两次拜谒星神。
t_en: In her senior years, she managed to reverse her own age via de-aging, and went on to solve the mystery of the imaginary leakage phenomenon, and even captured and sealed a stellaron. Madam Herta has saved the planet she was on from destruction <unbreak>19</unbreak> times and has met the Aeons twice.

t_ch: 这座空间站正是她的财产。
t_en: This space station is her property.

t_ch: 你看见空间站的标志，好像一把长枪穿过月亮。
t_en: You see the emblem of the space station. It looks like a spear piercing through the moon.

t_ch: 你看见空间站的标志，好像一只橡胶手臂握着吵闹的喇叭。
t_en: You see the emblem of the space station. It looks like a rubber arm holding a noisy horn.

t_ch: 你看见空间站的标志，好像一只耳机把鸡蛋砸破了洞。
t_en: You see the emblem of the space station. It looks like an earphone that has cracked a hole in an egg.

t_ch: 你看见空间站的标志，好像一只眼睛正在做眼压测试。
t_en: You see the emblem of the space station. It looks like an eye undertaking an intraocular pressure test.

t_ch: 你看见空间站的标志，好像一座空间站。
t_en: You see the emblem of the space station. It looks like a space station.

t_ch: 呵，以利亚萨拉斯，可爱的老头儿。他是天才俱乐部#56，俱乐部史上唯二的公选部长。感谢他吧！现代宇宙文明的交流基石——联觉信标和超距遥感都是他的创造。
t_en: Oh, Elias Salas, such a lovely old fellow. He is Member <unbreak>56</unbreak> of the Genius Society, one of the only two publicly elected Ministers in the Society's history. Be grateful to him! He created the Synesthesia Beacons and over-range remote sensing, the cornerstones of modern cosmic communication.

t_ch: ——虽然他没有公开发明，因此本人在此更正：这两项技术的版权属于「博识学会」。老实说，我不是很瞧得上学会，但她们能复刻以利亚萨拉斯的发明，也算成就斐然。
t_en: However, he did not disclose his inventions to the public, so I hereby certify that the rights of these two technologies belong to the Intelligentsia Guild. To be honest, I am not a big fan of the Guild, but it is impressive that they managed to reproduce Elias Salas' inventions.

t_ch: 波尔卡•卡卡目，天才俱乐部#4。一位充满神秘感的女人，俱乐部里有不少人陨于其手。很多年没有她的消息了，但我坚信她还活着。
t_en: Polka Kakamond, member #4 of the Genius Society. A woman full of mystery. Many people in the Society died by her hand. I haven't heard anything about her for many years, but I firmly believe she is still alive.

t_ch: 喂，「寂静领主」，如果你来到这里，如果你在听——我是#83黑塔，我想见你。相信你我会有很多共同话题。
t_en: Hey, Lord of Silence, if you're here, if you're listening... I'm member <unbreak>83</unbreak>, Herta, and I want to meet you. I believe you and I will have a lot to talk about.

t_ch: 赞达尔•壹•桑原，天才俱乐部#1。我挂上他的画像纯粹为了敬意：若没有他，也就没有我们如今的天才俱乐部。
t_en: Zandar One Kuwabara, Genius Society member 1. I hung up his portrait purely out of respect. Without him, there would be no Genius Society today.

t_ch: 说实话，我不知道他有什么成绩。他的著作和发明完全没有流传下来——但总要有个位置留给俱乐部的创始人，不是吗？
t_en: Frankly, I don't know what he has achieved. None of his books and inventions have survived, but there's gotta be a place for the founder of the Society, right?

t_ch: 利尔他，天才俱乐部#22。最无私的那一个，最奇怪的那一个。他把一辈子时间都花在帮助别人上，这种乐趣横竖我是感受不到。
t_en: This is Aiden, member <unbreak>22</unbreak> of the Genius Society. The most selfless and the strangest one. He spent his whole life helping others. Personally, I don't see the point.

t_ch: 但是拜他所赐，俱乐部才声名远扬。他过世后，星际和平公司一直想找到替代者。他们找了无数个琥珀纪，却没有一个天才愿意屈就…终于找到了我。
t_en: But the Society is famous thanks to him. The Interastral Peace Corporation has been trying to find a replacement ever since his death. They searched for many Amber Eras, but no genius was willing to lower their head to the IPC... Until they found me.

t_ch: 天才俱乐部#76螺丝咕姆。我认识他很久了，他是个真正的好人，一位绅士，拥有卓绝的机械头脑，和他的交流让我受益颇多……
t_en: Member <unbreak>76</unbreak> of the Genius Society, Screwllum. I have known him for a long time. He is a really good man, a gentleman with an exceptional mechanical mind. I have learned a lot from him.

t_ch: 最近我正和他、阮•梅合作推进一个项目！我希望，这个项目能够帮助我们接近宇宙的最大奥秘……
t_en: Recently, I have been working with him and Ruan Mei on a project! I hope this project can help us get closer to the greatest mystery of the universe...

t_ch: 这是谁？黑塔？
t_en: Who's this? Herta?

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yeah.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yeah.

t_ch: 这么年轻吗？我记得上一个琥珀纪她就很有名了，至少一百多岁吧？
t_en: She looks so young. She was already famous in the last Amber Era, no? She has to be at least a hundred years old.

t_ch: 她可是「天才俱乐部」的人。「智识」博识尊的令使。长生不老返老还童都不在话下吧。
t_en: She's a member of the Genius Society and an Emanator of Nous the Erudition. She can probably age backwards if she wanted.

t_ch: 她可是「天才俱乐部」的人。「智识」博识尊的令使。长生不老返老还童都不在话下吧。
t_en: She's a member of the Genius Society and an Emanator of Nous the Erudition. She can probably age backwards if she wanted.

t_ch: 呵，我记得俱乐部历史上八十多号人，大约一半都是寿终正寝的啊。不是还有个只活了十几天的…叫什么来着？
t_en: Hah. I seem to recall that over half of the Genius Society's eighty-odd members had a normal death. Wasn't there someone who only lived for a dozen days or so? What was their name...

t_ch: 这没什么奇怪的吧，游戏里那些长生不死的老怪，不总是等着主角来消灭他们吗？长生也不是什么好事。
t_en: That's pretty normal. Aren't the immortal bosses in games always waiting for the protagonist to come kill them? Immortality is not always a good thing.

t_ch: 这没什么奇怪的吧，游戏里那些长生不死的老怪，不总是等着主角来消灭他们吗？长生也不是什么好事。
t_en: That's pretty normal. Aren't the immortal bosses in games always waiting for the protagonist to come kill them? Immortality is not always a good thing.

t_ch: 哈，联盟笑话。
t_en: Hah. What an Alliance joke.

t_ch: 黑塔…你见过她吗？我很好奇拿自己的名字命名太空站的人品味如何，是高是低。
t_en: Herta... Have you ever met her? I'm curious about the tastes of someone who used their own name for a space station.

t_ch: 没见过真人，画像倒是见了不少次。
t_en: Never seen the real one. Saw plenty of her portraits, though.

t_ch: 可惜。
t_en: What a pity.

t_ch: 这白胡子老头儿是谁？
t_en: Who's this old guy with a white beard?

t_ch: 不认识，俱乐部的人？
t_en: No idea. A Society member?

t_ch: 不认识，俱乐部的人？
t_en: No idea. A Society member?

t_ch: …我知道这个人，她是「寂静领主」，波尔卡•卡卡目。你看，她的面部是空的，这人曾经在全宇宙范围内销毁了自己的画像和雕塑……
t_en: ...I know this person. She's the Lord of Silence, Polka Kakamond. See? Her face is all blank. She had destroyed all of her own portraits and sculptures in the entire universe...

t_ch: 我没兴趣啦，快去办正事。
t_en: I'm not interested. Hurry up with our real business.

t_ch: 我没兴趣啦，快去办正事。
t_en: I'm not interested. Hurry up with our real business.

t_ch: 这是赞达尔。赞达尔•壹•桑原，历史上第一位天才。
t_en: This is Zandar. Zandar One Kuwabara. The first genius in history.

t_ch: 那个传说中制造了博识尊的人？
t_en: The one who supposedly created Nous?

t_ch: 那个传说中制造了博识尊的人？
t_en: The one who supposedly created Nous?

t_ch: 是他…如果传说属实，那么他就是创造了星神的男人。
t_en: That's him... If the legends are true, then he was a man who created an Aeon.

t_ch: 最好传说不实，我可不想当赞达尔猎手。
t_en: Well they better be false. I don't want to be a Zandar hunter.

t_ch: 最好传说不实，我可不想当赞达尔猎手。
t_en: Well they better be false. I don't want to be a Zandar hunter.

t_ch: 这是谁？
t_en: Who's this?

t_ch: 不认识。
t_en: No idea.

t_ch: 不认识。
t_en: No idea.

t_ch: 嘿！银狼，看，是螺丝咕姆！你的老对手！
t_en: Hey! Silver Wolf! Look! It's Screwllum, your good old opponent.

t_ch: 哈…我说过了，那时我不知道是他……
t_en: Hahhhh... I told you already. I didn't know it was him...

t_ch: 哈…我说过了，那时我不知道是他……
t_en: Hahhhh... I told you already. I didn't know it was him...

t_ch: 就是那一战让艾利欧看上了你：宇宙里居然有人能破解螺丝咕姆的程序！…先是螺丝咕姆，再是黑塔，宇宙中怕是没有人在这么短的时间里连续招惹两个天才了，命运使然呢。
t_en: Ah, that battle was how Elio got his eyes on you. Imagine, someone actually able to crack Screwllum's code! First Screwllum, then Herta... there's probably no one else in the universe who offended two genii in such quick succession. It must be destiny.

t_ch: 唉…看来我才是「命运的奴隶」。
t_en: Urgh... Seems like I'm the real Destiny's Slave here.

t_ch: 唉…看来我才是「命运的奴隶」。
t_en: Urgh... Seems like I'm the real Destiny's Slave here.

t_ch: 欢迎来到黑塔空间站，请在此享受知识之海的馈赠——并自负溺亡的风险。
t_en: Welcome to Herta Space Station. Here, you can savor the bounty of the ocean of knowledge. Please also beware of the risk of drowning.

t_ch: 这提示一点用处也没有呀。
t_en: Now, this hint is useless.

t_ch: 谁知道追随「智识」的这帮人脑子里在想什么。
t_en: Who knows what's in the brains of those following the Erudition?

t_ch: 谁知道追随「智识」的这帮人脑子里在想什么。
t_en: Who knows what's in the brains of those following the Erudition?

t_ch: 光栅拦住了你的去路。
t_en: A galvanized gate blocks the way.

t_ch: 你不能通过！
t_en: You cannot pass!

t_ch: 如果您需要帮助，可以前往基座舱段的星语厅。
t_en: If you need help, go to the Galaxy Room in the base zone.

t_ch: 本月财务报销提醒通知：请各位科员将本月度的报销材料整理好，汇总到报销单上，时间节点分为两次。（如有加急单据，请单独联络对应科室）
t_en: This month's financial reimbursement reminder notice: Researchers, please summarize the reimbursement materials for this month on a single reimbursement form. (Please contact the corresponding section if you have any expedited documents)

t_ch: 黑塔空间站不会以押金、抵扣金等任何名义收取科员费用。如遇自称工作人员的科员向您索要个人信息，请及时报告防卫科。
t_en: Herta Space Station does not charge researchers fees for deposits, credits, etc. If someone claiming to be a staff member asks for your personal information, please report it to the Security Department.

t_ch: 部分黑屏的画面倒映出自己的脸，仔细看看还觉得挺有意思的。
t_en: The partially black screen reflects your face—you find it quite interesting as you look closer.

t_ch: ……你是在照镜子吗？
t_en: ...Are you checking yourself out?

t_ch: 黑色的屏幕中倒影出自己的脸，与其他鲜活灵动的画面形成鲜明的对比。
t_en: The black screen, which is reflecting your face, contrasts the vibrant and dynamic images.

t_ch: 啊，有时候就是这样的，在沉浸于虚拟的快乐当中的时候，画面突然一黑，倒映出自己的容貌。
t_en: Sometimes this is the way it is. Just as you are immersed in virtual bliss, the screen suddenly turns black and reflects your face back at you.

t_ch: 那个瞬间，就像是被某种力量，强行从快乐的虚拟，拽回到悲痛的现实。
t_en: At that moment, you are forced to return to sad reality from the wonderful virtual world.

t_ch: 真是残酷的黑屏。
t_en: What a cruel black screen.

t_ch: 不知道这么凌乱的桌面应该如何快速打开有用的信息。
t_en: I have no idea how to quickly find useful information on such a cluttered desktop.

t_ch: 也有可能对于这个空间站的科员而言，有用的信息全都不在电子存储设备里。
t_en: Perhaps none of the electronic devices on this space station have useful information for the researchers here.

t_ch: 不知道是哪位科员还在使用受损的屏幕进行工作呢？
t_en: I wonder who here has to work with a damaged screen?

t_ch: 他是否遇到了阻碍，是否遇到了压力，是否正遭遇更加惨痛的生活……
t_en: Have they encountered any pitfalls? Are they under stress? Are they experiencing an even more miserable life than me...?

t_ch: 想必他的工作就像是这个电子屏一样，已经出现了一定的耗能危机，却还在继续坚持吧。
t_en: I bet his work is like this screen... it may be having an energy-consuming crisis yet it is still insisting on working.

t_ch: 你怎么突然眉头紧皱？是想到了什么不好的事情吗？
t_en: Why are you suddenly frowning? Do you have something bad on your mind?

t_ch: 不知道是哪位科员还在使用受损的屏幕进行工作呢？
t_en: I wonder who here has to work with a damaged screen?

t_ch: 他一定不需要忙碌于复杂的工作吧，不然这个半吊子的屏幕怎么提供硬件支持？
t_en: I don't think he is busy with very complicated work, otherwise, how could this broken screen be of much use?

t_ch: 他肯定还是个热爱生活的人吧？不然这块不着调的电子屏幕是无法让人沉浸于愉快的虚拟世界当中的。
t_en: He must enjoy real life. There is no way that this unreliable screen could allow a person to be immersed in that pleasant virtual world.

t_ch: 你怎么突然面露微笑？是想到了什么开心的事情吗？
t_en: Why are you suddenly smiling? Did you think of something that makes you happy?

t_ch: 如遇外露的电线设施，请勿触碰，及时避行并通知防卫科。有条件的科员可就地设置简易路障，避免他人触碰。
t_en: Please do not touch exposed electrical wires. Avoid the route and notify the Security Department. Researchers who are in a position to do so can set up simple barricades around the area to prevent others from approaching.

t_ch: 请各位科员进行特殊放射性实验前，务必检索实验室的隔断功能是否处于开放状态。
t_en: Please make sure the isolation function of the lab is on before conducting radioactive experiments.

t_ch: 身份认证更新通知：本次更新支持义体机械ID代码以及虹膜扫描，欢迎各位科员申请办理。（佩戴接触性角膜无法使用虹膜扫描模式）
t_en: Identity Update Notice: The update supports prosthetic mechanical ID codes and iris scans. All researchers are welcome to apply. (Iris scan mode is not available for those who wear contact lens)

t_ch: 如果您正在遭遇：心烦意乱、精神涣散、皮肤粗糙——请尽快联系医疗舱室-心理健康科。
t_en: If you are experiencing anxiety, lack of motivation, or skin roughness, please contact the medical cabin's Mental Health Department as soon as possible.

t_ch: 如果您正在遭遇：无心工作、实验遇阻、科研受挫——请勿释放负能量影响其他科员。
t_en: If you are experiencing: Lack of motivation to work, setbacks in your experiments or frustration in your research, please do not allow your negativity to disturb other researchers.

t_ch: 屏幕旁边贴着一张小小的便利贴……
t_en: There's a small sticky note attached to the screen...

t_ch: 屏幕旁边贴着一张小小的告示……
t_en: There's a small announcement attached to the screen...

t_ch: 欢迎来到黑塔空间站，你在这里可以享受到知识之海的馈赠——但也有溺亡的风险，请多加小心。
t_en: Welcome to Herta Space Station. Here, you can savor the bounty of the ocean of knowledge. However, please also beware of the risk of drowning.

t_ch: 本月度商业保险理赔提醒通知：请于本月<unbreak>21</unbreak>号前提交线上理赔材料。
t_en: This month's commercial insurance claim reminder: Please submit your online claim by the <unbreak>21</unbreak>st of this month.

t_ch: 以下情况无法申请理赔：未携带空间站科员身份识别码就诊；未至空间站医务室进行就诊（私人手术室、独立医务室等非官方渠道难辨真伪，请各位科员擦亮眼睛）
t_en: Claims cannot be made in the following cases: Not carrying the space station member's identification code for medical treatment; not visiting the space station medical office (private surgeries, independent medical offices, and other unofficial channels are difficult to verify, so please be careful)

t_ch: 如遇自称科研人员的可疑人士邀请您加入他的奇物实验团队，并向您索要奇物研究押金，请务必谨慎，切记：不听不信不转账。
t_en: If you encounter a suspicious self-proclaimed researcher inviting you to his Curio research team and asking for an upfront deposit, please be careful and remember these words: Don't listen and don't transfer.

t_ch: HDH<unbreak>-125963</unbreak>  定分枪
t_en: HDH<unbreak>-125963</unbreak> Rating Pistol

t_ch: 只需将枪口处的眼球对准常见的物体，随后扣动扳机，即可收获定分枪给予的评分。
t_en: Simply aim the eyeball located at the muzzle at any everyday object and then pull the trigger to receive a rating from the gun.

t_ch: 暂无法解释其定分逻辑，目前仍在探索其评分的意义。
t_en: Rating logic currently unavailble. Significance of the rating cannot be evaluated.

t_ch: 「这个物件可以给人带来未知与具体交融的乐趣，具体来说就是——你永远不知道路过的那个人能得几分。是不是有点像开盲盒呢？」
t_en: "This object can give people the pleasure of intermingling with the unknown, more precisely — you'll never know how well that passerby might score. It's a bit like opening a mystery box, isn't it?"

t_ch: HDH<unbreak>-021879</unbreak>  螺中笑声
t_en: HDH<unbreak>-021879</unbreak> Crackup Conch

t_ch: 不断讲述遥远笑话的海螺，只需要将耳朵放置于螺口即可听见。
t_en: A conch that constantly tells jokes that are a bit out there. They can be heard by simply placing your ear at the mouth of the conch.

t_ch: 警告：请务必发笑，不可忍笑。
t_en: Warning: Try to laugh, don't ever hold back your laughter.

t_ch: 「它总能说出我想不到的幽默段子，可谓是居家旅行必备良品。建议压力大的科员都来听听。」
t_en: "It can always come up with a joke that's outside my wheelhouse. I think it's a must-have for a family road trip. I'd recommend it to any researcher that's under a lot of stress."

t_ch: 讲了笑话却没有人发笑，确实会很尴尬诶。
t_en: Well, telling jokes that no one finds funny is always quite awkward.

t_ch: ……这样的吗。
t_en: ... Is that so?

t_ch: HDH<unbreak>-369521</unbreak>  大剑的碎片
t_en: HDH<unbreak>-369521</unbreak> Greatsword Fragment

t_ch: 已证实该块碎片属于某把大剑的剑身。
t_en: This fragment has been confirmed to belong to the body of a certain greatsword.

t_ch: 内里似乎还蕴含着能量，有待进一步研究。
t_en: It seems to contain a certain kind of energy. Pending further research.

t_ch: 陈列柜被打碎了，里面已经空空如也。
t_en: The display cabinet has been shattered, and the contents emptied.

t_ch: 真没想到这么高端的空间站，奇物的陈列柜就是普通的玻璃，如此易碎。
t_en: Who would ever expect a display cabinet used for displaying Curios in such a high-tech space station to be made out of ordinary, fragile glass?

t_ch: 难道说……其实陈列柜本身装着的东西是假象，真正的奇物就是这个柜子？
t_en: Could it be that... the Curio inside the cabinet is a distraction, and the real Curio is the actual cabinet?

t_ch: 甚至于……那个奇物其实还在柜子中，并不为人所看见……？！
t_en: Or even... the Curio is actually still in the cabinet but it's just that no one can see it...!?

t_ch: 总感觉你盯着这个空柜子的眼神十分灼热。
t_en: Regardless, it seems like you're staring at the cabinet with a burning curiosity.

t_ch: 似乎是用于监控特殊死角的屏幕，并没有出现异常状态。
t_en: This screen seems to be used to monitor unique blind spots. Nothing abnormal is showing up.

t_ch: 部分监控画面处于黑屏状态，似乎需要输入特定ID才有权限查看对应录像。
t_en: Some of the windows are blacked out. It seems you need to enter a specific ID to gain permission to view the corresponding footage.

t_ch: 研究室的工作屏总是十分凌乱的，桌面中堆放着密密麻麻的杂乱文件，以至于壁纸图片都看不清了。
t_en: The display screen in the research room is always very messy. The wallpaper is entirely covered by a dense clutter of documents on the desktop.

t_ch: 仔细一看，竟然发现有一个图标酷似普通文档的游戏软件潜藏其中。
t_en: A closer look reveals a game software with an icon resembling an ordinary document lurking amongst the clutter.

t_ch: 屏幕进入待机状态，需要输入验证信息才可以使用该屏幕。
t_en: The screen is in standby. Verification is required before this screen can be used.

t_ch: 但在屏幕旁边贴着一张小小的便利贴……
t_en: But there's a small sticky note attached to the screen...

t_ch: 禁止在研究室外以科员身份对于光锥技术发表评论，禁止在研究室外以任何身份对流光忆庭发表评论。
t_en: It is forbidden to comment on Light Cone technology as a researcher outside the research laboratory. It is also forbidden to comment on the Garden of Recollection outside the research room under any identity.

t_ch: 画面不是十分清晰的屏幕，似乎是某些显示元件受到了损伤。
t_en: The image on the screen is not very clear. Some components of the display seem to be damaged.

t_ch: 可它依旧坚持不懈地在工作着，投射出短短的几个字母。
t_en: It is still working however, projecting a few short words.

t_ch: hello world
t_en: Hello World

t_ch: 如遇紧急情况，请各位科员立刻联系防卫科进行报备。
t_en: In case of emergency, all researchers are requested to contact the Security Department immediately.

t_ch: 如有科员因器官肢体受损，无法完成指纹或人脸信息识别，请联系防卫科进行特殊识别处理。
t_en: If any researchers cannot complete identification by fingerprint or face recognition due to limb and/or facial damage, please contact the Security Department for special identification.

t_ch: 如果您正在遭遇：失眠多梦、夜半盗汗、脱发头痛、食欲全无或食欲激增——请尽快联系医疗舱室-心理健康科。
t_en: If you are experiencing: Insomnia, night sweats, hair loss and headaches, total loss of appetite or a surge in appetite, please contact the medical cabin's Mental Health Department as soon as possible.

t_ch: 虽然是位于监控室的屏幕，但画面上并没有出现监控画面。
t_en: Despite this screen being located in the monitoring room, it's not displaying any form of surveillance footage.

t_ch: 反倒是一个文档页面占据了主要屏幕。
t_en: Instead, a page of text fills the screen.

t_ch: 综上所述，该监控室已遭到外部星系入侵。如果坚持使用该监控室权限访问内网，将会出现巨大风险。
t_en: In summary, this monitoring room has been hacked by invaders from an external star system. There will be a significant risk if you insist on using the extra permissions granted by the monitoring room to access the intranet.

t_ch: 故而本人申请暂停使用本监控室，同时申请本年度未使用带薪年假。
t_en: Therefore, I request to suspend the use of this monitoring room. Also, I'd like to go on holiday with the paid annual leave that I have left over this year.

t_ch: ……总感觉这个说法不是很靠谱的样子。
t_en: ... Something about that notice feels really fishy.

t_ch: 屏幕上布满了方形的监控画面，似乎并没有出现异常情况。
t_en: The entire screen is filled with surveillance screens, none of which show anything unusual.

t_ch: 但右下角似乎有什么东西在闪烁，任务栏好像还有个奇怪的图标……
t_en: Something is flashing at the bottom right corner and there is a strange icon in the taskbar...

t_ch: 点一下右下角
t_en: Click the bottom right corner

t_ch: 一个聊天窗口界面跳了出来。对方发来了数条消息。
t_en: A chat window pops up and several messages appear.

t_ch: 「你怎么挂机了？？？」「开团你人没了几个意思？」「出去就举报你！」
t_en: "Are you offline???" "How dare you go offline during an engagement!?" "I'm gonna report you!"

t_ch: 工作时间竟然玩游戏。
t_en: Gaming during working hours...?

t_ch: 哇，开团挂机，也太过分了吧！
t_en: Whoa, going offline during a game. That's unforgivable!
